children.value:
the linearized volume is preserved,
it is called a nearly incompressible  material. Then

children.html: λ≫μ\lambda \gg \muλ≫μ

children.value:
.
An example is rubber material, and also linearized problem from elasto-platicity
are nearly incompressible. These problems are ill-conditioned, and need robust, mixed
discretization methods.

Existence and uniqueness of the variational problem are proven by Lax-Milgram leamma.
Fundamential is

children.value: We see that Korn’s constant behaves badly on thin domains:

children.html: cK≤O(t2)c_K \leq O(t^2)cK​≤O(t2)

children.value: This is the motivation to study models on thin domains, as well as their limits on lower dimensional manifolds.
